relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Put away harm, deceit, hypocrisy, envy, and slander.
Long for pure word-milk and grow into healing.
Taste that the Lord is kind.
Come to the living stone, rejected yet precious.
Living stones become a spiritual house and holy priesthood.
The trusted cornerstone does not shame.
The rejected stone becomes the corner's head.
The same stone exposes stumbling.
Chosen lineage and royal priesthood proclaim marvelous light.
Those once not a people now receive mercy as Divine people.
Sojourners resist desires that war against life.
Beautiful conduct lets outsiders see and glorify the Divine.
Human institutions are navigated because of the Lord.
Authority is measured by justice and praise of good.
Doing good silences foolish ignorance.
Freedom is not cover for harm but service of Divine.
Honor all, love the siblinghood, revere the Divine.
Ancient household servants are addressed within unjust structures.
Unjust suffering is seen by the Divine.
Doing good under pressure is held before grace.
Messiah's suffering becomes a track of non-retaliatory trust.
No deceit was found in his mouth.
He did not return reviling, but entrusted himself to just judgment.
His wounded body opens healing and life toward justice.
Wandering sheep return to Shepherd and Overseer of life.

contemplative_note: 1 Peter 2 is living-stone identity and difficult household suffering language held with safeguards: freedom serves Divine, harm is not excused, and Messiah entrusts himself to just judgment.
